title: Ovarian Cancer Diagnostics Market Size to Reach USD 8.08 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global ovarian cancer diagnostics market is valued at USD 4.92 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 8.08 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 5.06%. Growth is driven by rising ovarian cancer incidence, increasing adoption of AI-powered imaging, biomarker testing, and precision oncology diagnostics. North America leads with 41.2% market share, while imaging and hospital laboratories dominate their respective segments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a leading market player with recent expansion of Cobas platform for CA-125 and HE4 assays, demonstrating active product development and market presence in ovarian cancer diagnostics.
Identified as a leading market player in the growing ovarian cancer diagnostics sector with significant market opportunities.
Listed as a major diagnostic firm with strong presence in North America's well-developed ovarian cancer diagnostics market.
Included among leading market players positioned to benefit from growing adoption of biomarker testing and precision oncology diagnostics.
Listed as a key player with potential to benefit from increased adoption of next-generation sequencing in ovarian cancer diagnostics.
Identified as a major diagnostic firm in North America with expertise in genetic testing, positioned to benefit from growing BRCA1/BRCA2 testing adoption.
Included as a leading player positioned to benefit from growing adoption of liquid biopsy technologies in ovarian cancer diagnostics.

title: Why Grail Could Be the Sleeper Stock Everyone's About to Notice
author: Keith Speights
description: Grail, a leader in multi-cancer early detection (MCED) testing, could be poised for significant growth as its Galleri test shows promising clinical trial results. The company awaits FDA approval and potential insurance reimbursement, which could unlock a $50 billion market opportunity. However, the stock remains high-risk due to unprofitability and competition from larger players.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positive clinical trial results from NHS Galleri 2 and Pathfinder 2 studies, FDA submission completed, potential Medicare coverage legislation enacted, and access to a $50 billion market opportunity. However, tempered by unprofitability and competition.
Mentioned as Grail's original parent company; no direct impact from Grail's developments discussed in the article.
Owns Exact Sciences which markets competing Cancerguard MCED test; competitive threat to Grail but no specific negative or positive developments mentioned.
Markets competing Shield MCED test; represents competitive pressure on Grail but no specific developments discussed.

title: AI-Powered Imaging Systems and Deep Learning Software Revolutionize Cancer Diagnostics Market Globally
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global AI in cancer diagnostics market is experiencing rapid growth, projected to expand from $0.79 billion in 2025 to $0.96 billion in 2026 (20.2% CAGR), reaching $1.97 billion by 2030. Growth is driven by advancements in deep learning, AI-powered imaging systems, rising cancer prevalence, and collaborations between AI developers and medical institutions. Key developments include Lunit's $193 million acquisition of Volpara and launches of integrated AI diagnostic systems by companies like Dedalus and Ibex Medical Analytics.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a prominent company shaping the AI cancer diagnostics market, indicating active involvement in this high-growth sector.
Identified as a key company in the expanding AI cancer diagnostics market with significant market presence.
Identified as a key company in the growing AI cancer diagnostics market.

title: Guardant Health Secures FDA Approval For Expanded Liquid Biopsy Test
author: Vandana Singh
description: Guardant Health received FDA approval for its Guardant360 Liquid CDx test, an expanded liquid biopsy panel that is now the largest FDA-approved in its category. The test evaluates a genomic footprint 100 times wider than the previous version, combines genomic and epigenomic insights from a single blood sample, and can deliver results in as little as seven days. The approval transfers seven previously approved companion diagnostic indications from the earlier Guardant360 CDx test. Shares rose 8.81% following the announcement.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: FDA approval of an expanded, more capable liquid biopsy test represents significant regulatory validation and competitive advancement. The test offers broader genomic coverage (100x wider), faster turnaround time (7 days), and integrated epigenomic insights. The stock price increased 8.81% on the news, reflecting positive market reception of this product expansion.

title: Global Colorectal Cancer Diagnostics and Therapeutics Market Anticipates Impressive Growth Trajectory at a CAGR of ~9% by 2034 | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The global colorectal cancer diagnostics and therapeutics market is projected to grow from USD 35 billion in 2025 to USD 73 billion by 2034 at a CAGR of ~9%. Growth is driven by rising colorectal cancer prevalence, increased screening awareness, advancements in non-invasive diagnostics (liquid biopsy, stool-based DNA tests), and emerging targeted therapies and immunotherapies. North America leads with 41% market share, while Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Key player in liquid biopsy and circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) analysis, which are highlighted as rapidly advancing non-invasive diagnostic technologies driving market expansion.
